steps.title: Use the zero from the point
steps.content:
The point $(-3,0)$ is on the graph, so $x=-3$ is a zero of $f$. In
$$
f(x)=(x-5)(x-9)(x+k)
$$
the zero from the last factor is $x=-k$.

steps.title: Find the constant
steps.content:
Set the last factor equal to $0$ at $x=-3$:
$$
-3+k=0
$$
So $k=3$.

steps.title: Evaluate the function
steps.content: Substitute $x=0$ and $k=3$: $$ f(0)=(0-5)(0-9)(0+3)=(-5)(-9)(3)=135 $$ The answer is D.
